title: Mexican Chicken Enchiladas (Stovetop)

subs.name: shredded rajma or soya chunks for a hearty vegetarian filling
thin wheat rotis can be rolled and used as enchilada wrappers
the chicken poaching liquid is perfect here — use it instead of stock
crumbled paneer mixed with a pinch of salt melts less smoothly but tastes good

steps.text: Place the chicken in a small pot with 1 cup water, half the garlic and a pinch of salt. Bring to a boil, reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked through. Remove, cool slightly and shred with two forks. Reserve the poaching liquid.

Blend the tomatoes, one onion (quartered) and the remaining garlic into a smooth sauce. Heat 1 tbsp oil in a kadai over medium flame, pour in the blended sauce and let it sizzle. Cook for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce darkens and thickens. Add 1 tsp chilli powder, cumin, coriander powder, oregano and salt, then stir in the stock. Simmer 3 more minutes.

Finely dice the second onion and sauté it in 1 tbsp oil until soft. Add the shredded chicken, remaining chilli powder and salt; stir together for 2 minutes until the filling is well seasoned and heated through.

Briefly dip each tortilla in the warm tomato sauce to soften it — about 5 seconds per side. Spoon 2–3 tbsp of chicken filling along the centre of each tortilla and roll it up tightly. Arrange all the rolled enchiladas seam-side down in a large wide kadai or frying pan.

Pour the remaining tomato sauce over the enchiladas and scatter the grated cheese on top. Cover the pan with a lid and cook on low flame for 8 minutes until the cheese melts and the tortillas are steamed through. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve directly from the pan.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → shredded rajma or soya chunks for a hearty vegetarian filling; tortilla → thin wheat rotis can be rolled and used as enchilada wrappers; cheese → crumbled paneer mixed with a pinch of salt melts less smoothly but tastes good; stock → the chicken poaching liquid is perfect here — use it instead of stock
indian_kitchen_adaptation: This stovetop version was specifically designed to work in an Indian kitchen without an oven. Use your regular kadai as the baking dish — a wide kadai with a tight-fitting lid is ideal. Thin wheat rotis are the perfect tortilla substitute. Amul cheese or any processed cheese melts easily and is available everywhere. The tomato-chilli sauce is very similar to a shahi gravy in technique.
